real_fix: Severity is correctly medium (degraded latency, not a correctness bug). Two acceptable fixes: (1) Document the degradation -- the header already says 'non-streaming', but add an explicit note in the plugin README / config docs that GeminiTTSPlugin is unsuitable for latency-critical v2v and exclude it from default low-latency presets, preferring deepgram/cartesia. (2) Better: add sentence-level batching by synthesizing on tts.text once a sentence boundary is buffered (split accumulated text on sentence-ending punctuation in the tts.text handler at index.ts:81-85, call synthesize() on each complete sentence, and only flush the remainder on tts.done). This converts TTFA from full-utterance latency to first-sentence latency without needing a streaming Gemini API. Given the @google/genai generateContent path has no token-streaming audio modality, batching is the practical ceiling. Recommend implementing the tts.text sentence-batch path and keeping the tts.done flush for the tail.
files: packages/voice-tts-gemini/src/index.ts:78-97 (accumulate-only tts.text + synthesize-on-tts.done), packages/voice-tts-gemini/src/index.ts:165-215 (single blocking generateContent, audio pushed only after full response). Reference contrast: packages/voice-tts-deepgram/src/index.ts:106-109 (per-delta streaming speak).
